92.reason: [Error: Resetting height to 1 after each insertion causes incorrect height calculation for subsequent nodes. The height should be tracked relative to the root, not reset globally.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Resetting height to 1 after each insertion causes incorrect height calculation for subsequent nodes. The height should be tracked relative to the root, not reset globally.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The global 'height' is reset to 1 after each insertion, but during recursive calls in 'input', it is incremented globally, causing interference between different insertion paths and incorrect height values. Height should be computed based on the current depth in the tree, not via a global variable.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Resetting height after insertion is too late; during recursive insertion, global 'height' was already misused, so resetting here doesn't fix incorrect height stored in nodes.]

58.reason: [Error: q is reassigned to left child, causing subsequent right child check to use left child's right instead of original node's right]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: q is reassigned to left child, causing subsequent right child check to use left child's right instead of original node's right]

61.reason: [Error: Due to previous modification, q is now the left child, so this condition checks left child's right child, not the original node's right]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Due to previous modification, q is now the left child, so this condition checks left child's right child, not the original node's right]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  q was reassigned to q->left in the previous block, so this condition checks the wrong node's right child. It should check middle->right.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Logical error: q was reassigned to q->left in the previous block, so this condition checks the left child's right pointer instead of the original node's right pointer. Should check middle->right.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [After traversing left subtree, 'q' may have been changed; using 'q->right' here is incorrect because 'q' no longer points to the original node. Should use 'middle->right'. However, note that the next line does use 'middle->right', so this condition should also use 'middle->right != NULL'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [After traversing left, 'q' may have been changed; using original 'middle' for right is correct, but condition should check 'middle->right' not 'q->right' which might be from left subtree. However, the bigger issue is inconsistent state due to prior height errors.]

17.reason: Incorrectly overwrites the height of an existing node p. The height of p is already set and should not be modified.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Redundant assignment: parent's height already correct; no error, but unnecessary.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrectly overwrites the height of an existing node p. The height of p is already set and should not be modified.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Logical error: incorrectly overwrites the parent node's height; the new node's height should be calculated as parent's height + 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This line incorrectly updates the parent's height (p->h) instead of only setting the new node's height. Also, 'height' is a global variable that gets reused across insertions, leading to incorrect height calculations.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This incorrectly updates the parent's height instead of the new node's height. Also, 'height' is a global variable that gets reused across insertions, leading to incorrect height calculations.]
